slides.title: One shared slide body
bullets: Presentation, Trainer, and Participant reuse the same content
Trainer and participant layers sit beside or below — they do not fork the slide copy
slides.emphasis: Single source of truth for what the room sees.
slides.trainerNotes: Settle the room. Name the three modes and where each role might sit—projector vs laptop vs phone.
promptQuestions: Where do you expect to use each mode this week?
What would distract you on the projector screen?
slides.transitionCue: When heads nod, advance—this is setup, not debate.
slides.interaction: With a partner: which mode will you use first when you rehearse this at home?

slides.title: Participant mode: reflection
bullets: Capture thoughts without changing the slide
Inputs are per-slide and local to this device
Export or sync can come later via the same state shape
slides.trainerNotes: Give 60–90 seconds of silence. Watch for people stuck—offer one starter phrase.
promptQuestions: Who heard something worth stealing from a neighbor?
slides.transitionCue: Invite one or two brief shares, then move on.
slides.interaction: In one or two sentences: what tension do you feel between partnership and “fundraising” language?

slides.title: Participant mode: fill-in
bullets: Same slide for audience; answers stay on participant devices
Great for conviction statements and reframes
slides.trainerNotes: Read the prompt once. Let them write before you comment—preserve their own wording.
slides.transitionCue: Optional: pair share using their completed sentence.
slides.interaction: Complete the sentences in your own words.
